Position Summary

The Assistant Manager Procurement will drive procurement activities for CDMO and Pharma Intermediates projects at Actylis, leveraging chemistry knowledge to source specialized raw materials and intermediates across the India/APAC/ROW region. The role focuses on managing tail spend (low-value, high-volume purchases) categories through supplier identification, qualification, and performance management, ensuring cost efficiency, quality compliance, and timely delivery. Key responsibilities include optimizing Total Cost of Ownership (TCO), coordinating with global teams, supporting negotiations, and ensuring effective supply chain execution for project needs.

Key Duties & Responsibilities

Support CDMO project procurement by sourcing chemical raw materials, intermediates, reagents, and consumables using chemistry knowledge. 
Identify, evaluate, and onboard new suppliers for tail spend and specialized chemical categories across the India/APAC region. 
Conduct supplier qualification and performance assessments in collaboration with Quality and Technical teams. 
Manage tail spend procurement through strategic consolidation, cost optimization, and process improvements. 
Establish and maintain a competitive Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) for assigned categories. 
Handle sourcing enquiries from global CDMO and Pharma Intermediates teams, ensuring timely and efficient responses. 
Support commercial negotiations, supplier contracts, and logistics coordination to ensure on-time project delivery. 
Collaborate with R&D, Process Development, and Manufacturing teams to align sourcing strategies with project and technical requirements. 
Monitor market trends, supply risks, and alternative sourcing opportunities related to chemical intermediates and CDMO operations. 
Continuously scan the India/APAC market to identify niche suppliers, new products, and alternative sources for competitive advantage. 

Education

BSC/MSC (Chemistry)/ BTech/MTech in Chemical Engineering or Graduate/Post-Graduate degree in Chemistry from reputed institutions
MBA in Supply Chain or Marketing is advantage but not mandatory

Experience

5 to 6 years in global sourcing & procurement role, with experience leading sourcing and logistics operations across Indian & global markets
Pharma intermediate and/or Specialty Chemicals industry and products supply chain experience
Strategic Sourcing knowledge and implementation experience
Understanding of the best global practices in the industry supply chain

Function: Finance

Reports To: Manager – Finance

Location: Head Office, Mumbai

Travel Required: 25% ☐ 50% ☐ 75% ☐

About DHL Blue Dart Express India:

At Blue Dart, we go beyond delivering shipments—we deliver trust, precision, and excellence. As India’s most reliable express logistics provider, we empower commerce by reaching over 56,000+ locations across India and connecting to 220+ countries through our global DHL network. Backed by the strength of DHL Group, we bring together international standards with deep local expertise. Our dedicated Boeing freighter fleet, 12,000+ vehicles, and best-in-class service ensure time-definite delivery that businesses and customers count on every day.

We are proud to be consistently ranked among India’s Top 100 Great Places to Work® and recognized as part of the DHL Group’s Top Employer certification across multiple regions. Being an Employer of Choice is not just a goal—it’s a fundamental part of who we are. We are committed to creating a workplace where people feel valued, empowered, and proud to belong.

If you're looking to join a purpose-led, people-first organization that shapes India’s supply chain with integrity and care—Blue Dart is where you belong.

Position Overview:

The Assistant Manager – Accounts (Finance) plays a key role in supporting billing governance, international revenue reconciliation, receivables management, and Order-to-Cash (OTC) control processes. The role ensures billing accuracy, compliance with contractual and regulatory requirements, timely reconciliation of international cost and revenue components, and structured MIS reporting to stakeholders.

The position requires close collaboration with Pricing, Revenue, Operations, Systems, and Regional Finance teams to ensure accurate billing execution and strong internal financial controls.

Job Purpose:

To ensure accurate and timely billing operations, disciplined receivable management, robust reconciliation controls, and compliance with accounting and regulatory frameworks — while supporting process automation and continuous improvement within the Order-to-Cash cycle.

Key Roles & Responsibilities:

International Billing & Revenue Reconciliation


Execute international billing for Trinity and related business streams. 
Perform cost versus revenue reconciliation for international operations. 
Process inbound and outbound international files ensuring completeness and accuracy. 
Ensure accurate billing of inbound and outbound duty taxes, including reconciliation of payables and receivables. 
Validate freight charges, value-added services, surcharges, and discounts prior to invoicing.


Billing Governance & Master Data Management


Update rate master’s for international business in coordination with Pricing. 
Maintain accurate customer billing profiles and master data.

Position

Manager

Department

Warehouse Operations

Location

NSBP – Operations

Position reports to

Senior Manger Operations

Roles & Responsibilities

Purpose of the Role

The Warehouse Manager is responsible for leading and managing all day‑to‑day warehouse operations to ensure efficient receipt, storage, handling, and dispatch of goods. The role ensures operational excellence, safety, inventory accuracy, resource planning, and compliance with organizational standards and statutory requirements. The position also drives continual improvement to enhance productivity, service levels, and cost efficiency.

Key responsibilities

A. Warehouse Operations Management
Oversee inbound, outbound, and inventory management activities.
Ensure timely unloading, put‑away, picking, packing, and dispatch operations.
Develop and implement standard operating procedures (SOPs) for smooth workflows.
Coordinate closely with transport, procurement, and customer service teams.
B. Inventory & Space Management
Monitor inventory accuracy through cycle counts, audits, and reconciliation.
Ensure optimal space utilization and layout planning within the warehouse.
Investigate and resolve inventory discrepancies promptly.
C. People Leadership
Lead a team of supervisors, executives, and warehouse associates.
Plan manpower deployment based on workload (shift planning & rostering).
Conduct training, performance reviews, and skill development programs.
D. Safety, Quality & Compliance
Ensure adherence to safety standards, 5S, and housekeeping guidelines.
Maintain compliance with customs, excise, and statutory requirements (if applicable to SEZ/EZ operations).
Investigate and record warehouse incidents and drive corrective actions.
E. Process Improvement & Reporting
Identify opportunities to reduce cost and improve operational KPIs.
Generate daily, weekly, and monthly MIS reports.
Coordinate for internal and external audits.
Preferred Qualities:
Ability to work independently and ensure compliance with stringent regulatory guidelines, particularly for cold chain management.
Proactive and problem-solving mindset with strong communication skills.
Prior experience in managing cold chain audits and inspections.
Work Conditions:
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with a high level of accuracy.
Physically capable of handling and moving products, particularly cold chain items that may require special equipment.
Comfortable working in cold chambers for extended periods, ensuring proper PPE and safety measures are followed.
QUALIFICATIONS & COMPETENCIES 

Qualification & Competencies

Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm) or B.Sc. Microbiology or Any bachelor’s degree.

What Role You Will Play In Team
The Contracts Manager will be responsible for managing the full contract lifecycle, optimizing Total Cost of Ownership (TCO), and ensuring strong supplier and stakeholder alignment across categories. This role provides, commercial oversight, governance cadence management, and operational rigor, ensuring that contracting outcomes consistently deliver value, mitigate risk, and support category and business strategies. Work closely with, internal stakeholders, Legal, and Supply partners to drive performance, streamline contracting processes, and ensure high-quality execution across pre award, award, and post award contract lifecycle stages.

Job will be based at Bangalore, Whitefield office ( WFO) for 5 days in a week.

Key Responsibilities
What you will do 

Commercial Delivery & Quality Assurance
Oversee and review all major negotiations to ensure commercial excellence. 
Validate pricing structures, terms & conditions, performance metrics, service levels, and risk allocation. 
Ensure agreements fully reflect business needs, category guidance, and global procurement standards. 
Support development of negotiation strategies, bid evaluations, and award recommendations. 
Contract Lifecycle Ownership & Governance
Ensure full contract lifecycle in alignment with corporate procedures (Requisition-to-Pay, MOC, stewardship). 
Maintain governance over scopes, pricing updates, exhibits, amendments, and supplier performance mechanisms. 
Oversee contract compliance and ensure supplier performance monitoring, issue resolution, and utilization tracking. 
Support standardization and efficiency of contracting processes, tools, and templates. 
Category Strategy & Business Alignment
Partner with Category Managers to translate category strategy into contracting plans and execution roadmaps. 
Align team activities with Category commercial and strategic guidance, market intelligence, and risk outlook. 
Identify opportunities to consolidate demand, optimize contracting models, and enhance value across categories. 
Provide commercial insights to drive cost avoidance, cost mitigation, and lifecycle value. 
Supplier Relationship & Performance Oversight
Ensure strong supplier engagement and drives value in negotiations and performance management. 
Provide escalation support for supplier issues, performance gaps, stewardship, or commercial disputes. 
Ensure actively track performance trends and utilize insights to inform contract changes or renewals. 
Risk Management & Compliance
Ensure adherence to corporate policies, legal requirements, and internal controls across all agreements. 
Partner with Legal and Risk to ensure contract language, liability frameworks, and operational requirements are aligned with corporate standards. 
Mitigate supply and legal risk through rigorous review of commercial terms and supplier commitments. 
Operational Excellence & Continuous Improvement
Lead the improvement of contracting processes, templates, guidelines, and tools. 
Drive consistency reduce cycle time and increase contracting quality. 
Support implementation of digital tools, sourcing platforms, and data-driven insights for better decision-making.

GE Vernova – Grid Systems Integration product line is globally recognized for designing, manufacturing, and delivering customized High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) solutions for utilities worldwide. With over 130 years of pioneering experience, GE offers two HVDC technologies, Line Commutated Converters (LCC) and Voltage Source Converters (VSC), to support a broad range of applications and deployable in different schemes including overhead line (point to point), back-to-back, submarine/land cable and offshore.

Every HVDC solution is tailored and designed based on a project-by-project assessment of the customers' individual requirements, whether it's for connecting offshore wind generation, long distance power transmission, or energy trading between independent networks.

This tender engineer role will be focusing initially deploying tenders tools across ITO (Inquiry to Order) organizations in the region and PDGs (Engineering, supply chain, proposal manager and construction) . The tender engineer to lead wing to wing tools adoption following processes

The role is expected to demonstrate technical leadership, team work capability and the ability to keep up with evolving technologies in GSI domain, versatile in adapting new product platforms and mentor the junior members of the team and other colleagues. In this role, you will utilize your experience or expertise to solve problems, develop and execute team and self objectives, and possess the ability to effect short-term and some long-term business goals.

The Tender Engineer/ will play a key role in supporting the sales and business development by providing technical expertise in the design of electrification solutions for the HVDC & EHV/HV Substations. This individual will be responsible for system design, material selection, quantity estimation, various engineering activities associated for a tender and customer technical consultations. The candidate will also ensure that the proposed solutions meet customer needs while adhering to industry standards, safety regulations, quality requirements and cost-efficiency objectives.

The candidate shall execute with association of workgroups, where applicable, in order to meet assigned objectives. In this role, the candidate will work within defined parameters to make decisions, apply concepts to issues of moderate complexity, and resolve issues through immediate action or short-term planning.

The candidate will work with GSI Global Engineering Center (GSI GEC) to coordinate with the GSI Regions / Business Lines / as per Project requirement.

Job Description

Roles and Responsibilities

Specific Role Requirements

ITO Configurator (ITOC) Tool deployment :

ITO Configurator tool is collaborative tendering tool use by all functions (Engineering, sourcing, proposal team) to enable to deliver an offer following our technical solutions.

Provide technical support/methods and specific technical configuration to the team to enable tool usage (ACS/HVDC/FACTS).

Provide functional & cross-functional support as required by region and PDG. (Functions involved engineering, proposal team and sourcing team.)

Understand the engineering process Inquiry to Order (ITO) to Order to Remittance (OTR), to ensure Bill of Material Bill of Quantity (BOQ) through ITOC done

Ensure region quality of data in tool.

From input of Standard solution team develop and support HVDC standard solution in ITOC tool. Support for maintenance of standard solution

Act as focal point for functional support, 1st and 2nd level. Support local key user and large tenders teams to use ITOC tool for all tenders. Organize the training to key users on the functional use of the tool

Maintain reference/training documentation for all user categories and functions (engineering, proposal team, sourcing and constructions)

Technical Support For Sales & Business Development

Provide expert technical advice during the sales process to ensure solutions align with client needs and specifications.

Participate in pre-sales meetings with customers, acting as the technical lead to discuss system requirements, feasibility, and potential customizations.

Collaborate with the sales team to develop competitive proposals, ensuring accurate technical descriptions, design insights, and cost-effective solutions.

System Design & Engineering

Lead the design of electrical systems, including power distribution networks, substations, and integration of renewable energy sources tailored

Evaluation of vendor Drawings, GTP, Calculation & Technical Specs

LV/MV Engineering for the plant & substation

ITO (tender) drawings, BOQ estimation, RFQ finalization, evaluation of various components & selection of equipment.

Co-ordination and integration with Automation, Civil & MEP.

Co-ordination of site & office team for execution of project

With a presence in 500+ cities across India, partnerships with hundreds of thousands of restaurants, an employee base of over 5000, a 2 lakh+ strong independent fleet of Delivery Executives, we deliver unparalleled convenience driven by continuous innovation. Built on the back of robust ML technology and fuelled by terabytes of data processed every day, Swiggy offers a fast, seamless and reliable delivery experience for millions of customers across India.

From starting out as a hyperlocal food delivery service in 2014, to becoming India’s leading on-demand convenience platform today, our capabilities result not only in lightning-fast delivery for customers, but also in a productive and fulfilling experience for our employees.

POSITION VACANT: Senior Procurement Manager – Museum, Exhibitions & Marketing, Kiran Nadar Museum of Art (KNMA), New Delhi

2. ORGANIZATION BACKGROUND:

Our client, the Kiran Nadar Museum of Art (KNMA), is a pioneering institution in the world of Indian art and culture. Established in 2010 at the initiative of avid art collector Kiran Nadar, KNMA holds the distinction of being India's first private museum dedicated to modern and contemporary art from the subcontinent.

An entity of the Shiv Nadar Foundation, KNMA operates as a non-commercial, not-for-profit organization with a mission to bridge the gap between art and the public. The institution is a key driver in the effort to cultivate a broader museum-going culture and has become a vital hub for artistic confluence, intellectual dialogue, and cultural exploration.

The museum's core philosophy centers on creating a dynamic relationship between art and society. This is achieved through a robust calendar of critically acclaimed exhibitions, scholarly publications, and extensive public programming. KNMA fosters a culture of innovation and engagement, with a strong emphasis on education through workshops, symposiums, and collaborations with schools and other community organizations.

For more information about KNMA, please visit https://www.knma.in/ 

3. JOB DESCRIPTION/ RESPONSIBILITIES:

Reporting to the Group Manager – Procurement, the Senior Procurement Manager – Museum, Exhibitions & Marketing (SPM-MEM) will lead procurement across KNMA’s museum operations, exhibitions, and marketing functions. The role will focus on strengthening the vendor ecosystem for niche museum requirements, ensuring timely sourcing for exhibitions and installations, and overseeing marketing procurement, with particular emphasis on BTL categories and annual ATL contract renewals. 

The incumbent will work closely with the curatorial, exhibitions, design, marketing, and outreach teams to deliver efficient, compliant, and cost-effective procurement outcomes.
Key Job Responsibilities:

(A) Vendor Base Development: 
(1) Build and expand a robust vendor network for niche museum needs, fabrication, art logistics, AV systems, event management and BTL marketing;
(2) Identify, evaluate, and onboard new suppliers to reduce single-vendor dependency;
(3) Maintain an up-to-date vendor database supported with relevant card-rates for recurring activities with performance tracking.

(B) Museum & Exhibition Procurement: 
(1) Build vendor base for art handling, packing, conservation materials, exhibition supplies, fabrication, printing, lighting, AV systems, documentation, display infrastructure and local and international logistics;
(2) Coordinate with curatorial, exhibitions, design, and technical teams to forecast requirements and lead seamless procurement for build-outs and installations.

(C) Marketing, Educational Outreach and Cultural Events Procurement: 
(1) Lead procurement for all museum events and outreach programmes including venue setup, AV systems, indoor and outdoor signage, promotional material printing, art material for workshops, experiential elements, catering services, and on-ground installations;
(2) Oversee procurement for BTL marketing categories, including digital asset production and influencer-led activations;
(3) Standardise rate cards, diversify and strengthen vendor options, and optimise spending across categories;
(4) Manage the annual ATL agency contract through benchmarking, commercial evaluation, scope assessment, and performance review.

(D) Procurement Planning, Operations & Governance:
(1) Develop procurement plans aligned with exhibition calendars, programme schedules, and marketing timelines;
(2) Manage tenders, RFQs, POs, and all procurement documentation as per institutional procedures;
(3) Draft and review contracts for services, fabrication, logistics, media, and art transport, in coordination with legal and finance;
(4) Ensure compliance with institutional policies, sustainability guidelines, and financial controls;
(5) Provide cost insights, vendor recommendations, and market benchmarks during planning and budgeting;
(6) Conduct price benchmarking, supplier capability assessments, competitive bidding, and e-auctions to enhance efficiency and cost effectiveness;
(7) Track industry trends in exhibition production, art logistics, and marketing procurement to inform category strategies.

4. QUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE AND COMPETENCIES:

(1) Minimum 10 years of relevant procurement experience, preferably across museums, exhibitions, cultural institutions, or allied environments such as large-scale events, experiential marketing, luxury retail, hospitality, media, design, architecture, or other project-based environments involving specialised fabrication and service procurement.

About Us - Malvern Panalytical, Micromeritics And SciAps.

Together we are a powerful and highly complementary combination of market leading technologies.

We are the toolmakers for the world's most innovative companies, academic institutions, and government laboratories. We are their partners in discovery.

Malvern Panalytical instruments analyze the chemical, physical and structural nature of materials, from proteins to polymers and semiconductors to minerals. Our leading technologies measure particle size, shape, concentration and zeta potential, biomolecular interactions and stability, elemental concentrations and crystallographic structure.

Micromeritics manufactures systems for the characterization of particles, powders, and porous materials for a wide- range of end markets including catalysts, chemicals, building materials, clean-tech and battery. Our leading technologies measure surface area, porosity, density, adsorption and particle activity.

SciAps specializes in portable X-ray fluorescence (XRF), laser-based (LIBS) and near-infrared (NIR) analyzers to measure any element in any environment. SciAps is the Center of Excellence for our handheld instruments.

We have a global footprint with R&D and manufacturing sites in North America, Europe, and Asia.

Role Overview

As part of METRO’s Global Solution Center, India, you are responsible for delivering accurate customer demand forecasts within METRO and enhancing them with market intelligence through cross-functional collaboration. This role ensures alignment across company functions by managing consensus on forecasts and driving continuous improvement in forecast accuracy through exception management and parameter updates. Operating within company policies and best practices, the Demand Planner plays a key role in decision-making and process improvement initiatives, working closely with the Supply Chain Planning Manager to optimize demand planning performance.

Key Responsibilities

Operational:

Ensure timely and accurate demand forecasts for assigned articles and categories.
Analyze and cleanse historical sales data to establish a solid foundation for forecasting.
Generate statistical forecasts for short-, mid-, and long-term horizons (up to 52 weeks).
Forecast sales for new product introductions.
Calculate and refine promotional uplift in collaboration with other functions (e.g., Category Managers) to accurately forecast promotional sales.
Review and analyze forecast results for regular assortment, promotional articles, and future events.
Manage exceptions identified by forecasting tools, focusing on key assortments (e.g., high-rotation, new, seasonal, promotional, competitive, and top-customer articles) and take corrective actions, involving other functions as needed.
Ensure proper aggregation and reconciliation of forecasts across all levels (article, category, store, time) in both units and value.
Continuous improvement:

Identify opportunities for improvement and implement solutions.
Analyze recurring exceptions and propose actions to minimize them.
Fine-tune forecasting algorithms and parameters to enhance accuracy.
Maintain Master Data in system


Collaboration:

Work closely with Category Managers, Department/Store Managers, and Sales teams to optimize customer service.
Gather market-related information from other functions (e.g., promotions, D&Rs, new article introductions) to support forecasting.
Incorporate market intelligence into demand forecasts.
Lead cross-functional demand planning meetings to achieve consensus on sales expectations.
Challenge sales expectations from offer and operations teams through in-depth analysis of historical sales data.

Role Summary

The Consolidation Manager / Lead is responsible for end-to-end planning, execution, and optimization of international shipment consolidation across EMEA, APAC, South East Asia, and India. The role focuses on building cost-efficient, compliant, and scalable consolidation models to support reliable inbound supply flows.
This position requires strong expertise in setting up consolidation centres, managing global trade compliance, and ensuring documentation accuracy across countries with diverse regulatory requirements. The role works in a partner-led environment, collaborating with consolidation partners, first-mile logistics providers, freight forwarders, customs brokers, factories, overseas QA agencies, and internal supply chain, compliance, and finance teams.

Key Responsibilities

Cargo Consolidation Planning & Execution

Plan and manage weekly/monthly consolidation across sourcing regions including China, Vietnam, Cambodia, Bangladesh, Indonesia, Thailand, India, and others
Identify opportunities to combine shipments, maximize FCL/LCL utilization, and reduce freight and handling costs while meeting compliance requirements
Coordinate with vendors to ensure cargo readiness aligned to pickup schedules and consolidation cut-offs
Review packaging, labelling, and carton configurations to improve consolidation efficiency and compliance

Consolidation Partner & Forwarder Coordination

Act as the primary point of contact for consolidation partners, first-mile providers, global forwarders, and origin agents
Review and enforce SOPs, routing guides, SLAs, and cost targets
Monitor shipment execution, vessel/flight schedules, origin handling, and transshipment risks
Ensure timely and accurate pre-alerts, stuffing reports, VGMs, manifests, and milestone updates
Onboard and scale consolidation partners across sourcing geographies

Global Trade Compliance & Regulatory Alignment

Ensure compliance with country-specific trade regulations, including HS classification, labelling and fibre content, COO rules, restricted product lists, and import licenses (where applicable)
Maintain working knowledge of regulations across GCC, ASEAN, EU, India, and North America
Partner with internal product compliance and business teams to ensure regulatory readiness prior to shipment

Destination Documentation & Customs Compliance

Ensure completeness and accuracy of shipping and customs documentation (CI, PL, COO, test reports, inspection certificates, HS codes, FTA certificates, packing declarations)
Coordinate with destination customs brokers to prevent clearance delays and resolve queries
Maintain documentation checklists, pre-audit controls, and recordkeeping to support BOE filing and audits Cost Optimisation & Vendor Performance Management
Evaluate freight rates, consolidation charges, origin handling costs, and value-added services
Drive cost savings through better consolidation, mode optimisation (air/sea), error reduction, and partner negotiations
Develop and track partner scorecards covering cost, timeliness, documentation accuracy, and service reliability

Internal Stakeholder Collaboration

Work closely with Merchandising, Buying, QA, Finance, EXIM, Operations, and Warehousing teams
Provide visibility on consolidation status, in-transit shipments, risks, and exceptions
Support PO execution planning, especially during peak seasons and urgent replenishments

Reporting, Analytics & KRAs

Maintain dashboards tracking container utilization, cost performance, compliance accuracy, and consolidation cycle times
Report exceptions impacting shipment timelines and inbound performance
Ensure high compliance accuracy, complete documentation, and timely inwarding in coordination with SCM teams

Requirements

9–14 years of experience in international logistics, consolidation management, or EXIM operations—preferably in softlines/apparel, footwear, accessories.
Hands-on experience managing cargo flow across China and Southeast Asian sourcing hubs.
Strong understanding of:
Trade compliance across multiple geographies in SEA and GCC
Destination customs processes (India and SEA preferred, but GCC/EU/US exposure is an advantage)
Global forwarding operations, CFS operations, FCL/LCL consolidations
Import documentation and BOE requirements
Experience working with large-scale consolidation partners and freight forwarders.
Prior exposure to retail, apparel manufacturing, or lifestyle categories is highly desirable.

Global trade compliance & regulatory understanding
HS classification familiarity

Supply Chain Channel Manager – Online B2B Customers

Job Description

The Supply Chain Channel (Account) Manager is responsible for the end-to-end lifecycle of our online B2B customer segment. This role sits at the intersection of sales and operations, managing everything from initial Order-to-Delivery (OTD) execution to complex claims settlement. You will be the primary advocate for our digital partners, ensuring that supply planning aligns with demand and that contractual service levels are met through data-driven decision-making.

Your Role

Order Management & Execution:
Oversee the full Order-to-Delivery (OTD) cycle for online B2B accounts, ensuring seamless integration between the web portal and the ERP.
Monitor order flow and lead times to ensure ""Perfect Order"" fulfillment (Right Product, Right Time, Right Condition).
Build and maintain strong relationships with key customers, understanding their supply chain needs and business objectives.
Guides the development of new and complex Service Level Agreements (SLAs), collaborating with stakeholders to ensure alignment with evolving customer needs and organizational objectives, thereby enhancing service quality and reliability.
Supply Planning & Integration:
Collaborate with the Demand Planners to provide insights into online B2B trends, seasonal spikes, and promotional impacts.
Ensure inventory availability for key digital accounts by influencing Supply Planning priorities and safety stock levels.
Anticipate potential supply chain disruptions and proactively implement contingency plans.
Contract & Stakeholder Management:
Interpret and enforce Customer Contracts, ensuring all supply chain-related clauses (delivery windows, packaging specs, penalties) are operationalized.
Act as the lead liaison between Sales, Finance, Logistics, and the Customer's procurement teams.
Identify and resolve supply chain issues impacting key accounts promptly and effectively.
Represents the organization in customer interactions, ensuring proactive communication and accurately reflecting customer feedback and requirements, thereby fostering strong customer relationships and loyalty.
Escalation & Claims Settlement:
Serve as the primary point for Escalation Management, resolving critical bottlenecks in the delivery network.
Manage the end-to-end Claims Settlement process for shortages, damages, or pricing discrepancies, ensuring fair resolution and root-cause analysis to prevent recurrence.
Analytics & Digital Performance:
Build and maintain automated dashboards in PowerBI to track Channel KPIs (e.g., Fill Rate, On-Time In-Full, Cost-to-Serve).
Utilize Advanced Excel to conduct deep-dive analysis on order patterns and logistics costs to optimize channel profitability.
Monitors and reports on key performance indicators and operational metrics to internal stakeholders and customers, providing valuable insights and recommendations for further improvement and optimization of business operations.
Education

You're the right fit if:

MBA in Supply Chain Management, Operations, or Business from a Tier‑1 or Tier‑2 management institutes.

Experience

Minimum 7 years of experience in Supply Chain or Logistics, with strong exposure to B2B E‑commerce or Account Management.

Industry Experience

Prior experience in E‑commerce, FMCG, FMCD, or Quick Commerce is essential, with a strong understanding of fast‑moving supply chains, high‑volume order fulfillment, and digitally integrated customer operations.

Technical Expertise

Power BI: Advanced proficiency in data modeling, dashboard creation, and visualization.
Excel: Expert-level skills, including Power Query, complex formulas, and data cleansing.
ERP Systems: Hands-on experience with SAP or Oracle (preferred).
Planning Tools: Familiarity with platforms such as Blue Yonder, JDA, or O9.

Negotiation

Proven ability to manage customer contracts and lead high‑value financial claims to resolution.

Communication

Strong capability to navigate cross-functional dynamics and communicate supply constraints effectively to high-priority customers.

How We Work Together

We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.

Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.

Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.

This is an office based role.

Inventory & Stock Management

Define and align stocking norms with markets, considering sales velocity, seasonality, safety stock requirements, and replenishment lead times.

Ensure smooth flow of materials from production to markets.

Sales loss due to stockouts
Inventory availability and coverage
Forecast / demand accuracy
Supply adherence and OTIF (On-Time-In-Full) performance

S&OP Process Management

Position Title: Supply Chain Manager

Reporting to: Operations Manager

Business: Automotive & Industry

Location: Chakan, Pune

Overview

The Supply Chain Manager will lead end‑to‑end supply chain operations for our Automotive & Industry business, ensuring uninterrupted material availability, optimizing cost, and enhancing delivery performance across global and domestic supplier bases. This role manages buying, logistics, inventory, supplier relationships, Customer Services, and demand planning while supporting new product launches and sustainability goals. The ideal candidate has deep experience in automotive supply chains, strong analytical skills, and a track record of operational excellence.

Roles and Responsibilities:

Supply Chain Strategy & Planning
Develop and implement supply chain strategies aligned with production schedules, SOP timelines, and annual business goals. 
Lead demand forecasting and capacity planning to ensure supply readiness for both current and future vehicle programs. 
Lead complete S&OP Process. 
Drive risk assessment and mitigation plan for critical product and build strong Business Continuity and Contingency Plan. 
Drive Capacity Planning process and derive Capacity Constraints and enhancement plan to meet pipeline Business requirement. 
Buying & Supplier Management
Manage sourcing strategies for global inter-company and local and global third-party suppliers, ensuring timely delivery, cost competitiveness and quality compliance. 
Understand global trade practices and derive benefits from same. 
Evaluate supplier performance using KPIs such as OTD (On‑Time Delivery), PPM (Parts Per Million), warranty impact, and logistics scorecards. 
Negotiate contracts, long-term agreements, and cost reduction initiatives while maintaining strong supplier partnerships. 
Close coordination for Product Transitions and continuity. 
Inventory & Materials Management
Optimize inventory levels for raw materials, consumables, and finished goods using lean and JIT principles based on Leadtime. 
Ensure Customer supplies without any disturbance and proactive planning. 
Oversee MRP processes, safety stock calculations, and demand-supply balancing. 
Minimize obsolescence and excess inventory through lifecycle tracking and engineering change coordination. 
Logistics & Distribution
Understand requirements from Business and Customer to create stronger distribution channel. 
Lead inbound and outbound logistics operations, ensuring efficient movement of all Product lines. 
Work closely with 3PL/4PL partners, transporters, and warehouse teams for cost, speed, and reliability improvements. 
Implement logistics optimization projects, including route planning, consolidation, and export compliance for global shipments. 
Customer Service
Drive Complete Order Management process for maximizing customer satisfaction. 
OTIF compliance
Close coordination with Customer on understanding specific requirements. 
Lead Claim Management process for faster Customer issue resolution on Supply Chain related topics. 
Monitor forecast vs offtake variance of key customer accounts and lead discussion with customer to keep variances, if any, within agreed SLA
Cross-Functional Collaboration
Collaborate with Production, Engineering, Quality, Finance, and Program Management to ensure smooth daily operations and launch readiness. 
Support new product development (NPD) by coordinating procurement and ramp-up planning. 
Facilitate resolution of supply disruptions, quality issues, and engineering changes. 
Data, Systems & Compliance
Ensure data accuracy across ERP/MRP systems and drive digitalization initiatives (RFID, dashboards, automation). 
Monitor compliance with automotive standards such as IATF 16949, ISO 9001, ISO14001, and regulatory import/export guidelines. 
Prepare and present timely supply chain reports to leadership. 
Competencies

Functional

Strong expertise in MRP/ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, or equivalent). 
Excellent negotiation, vendor management, and analytical skills. 
Strong understanding of automotive production processes, PPAP, BOM management, and quality systems VDA, IATF, ISO etc. 
Good Knowledge of working with Automotive and Industrial Customers like Maruti, Tata, SML etc. 
Knowledge of lean methodologies, Kanban, and continuous improvement tools. 
Familiarity with global sourcing and import/export regulations. 
Behavioural

Good communication skills. 
Team worker with the ability to influence cross-functional teams

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Supply chain, Operations, Engineering, Business, or related field
MBA or relevant Master’s preferred
Min. 10 years of supply chain experience (automotive industry highly preferred)
Certifications (CSCP, CPM, APICS, Lean Six Sigma) are an advantage

Communication

Clearly and succinctly conveying information and ideas to individuals and groups in a variety of situations; communicating in a focused and compelling way that drives others’ thoughts and actions. Engages the audience and helps them understand and retain the message.

Continuous Improvement

Originating action to improve existing conditions and processes; identifying improvement opportunities; generating ideas, and implementing solutions.

Continuous Learning

Actively identifying new ideas for learning; regularly creating and taking advantage of learning opportunities; using newly gained knowledge and skill on the job and learning through their application.

Customer Focus

Ensuring that the customer perspective is a driving force behind business decisions and activities; crafting and implementing service practices that meet customers’ and own organization’s needs.

Job Purpose

The Global Delivery Centre provides the critical procurement service capability for GSK. Leading the delivery of a high volume of sourcing projects globally, the centre acts as the operational engine of the procurement organisation. As such, the GDC will play a critical role in delivering both the in-year savings and the functions long term operating model.

The GDC SLM_Ops Sr. Manager will be accountable for ensuring seamless delivery of the critical Source to Contract service globally for GSK. This role works collaboratively with the procurement category and COE teams to design and manage the processes required to successfully operate GDC, cadence of procurement performance on Key KPIs to global leadership teams, designing and executing talent management plan and continuous improvement plan for GDC. This service is a cornerstone of the future procurement operating model and will enable the function to industrialise its core operation; driving best practice, providing practical knowledge for development of best in class category strategies, consistency of execution, and continual improvement.

The GDC SLM_Ops Sr. Manager will be responsible for ensuring high performance from a team of 50+ people, through delivering effective performance improvements and trainings programs. This role requires: strong understanding of procurement concepts and processes, intelligence/maturity on softer capabilities required to run large service delivery teams, expertise in identifying capability gaps and converting them in high impact training programs. This role also requires strong understanding of strategic objectives of procurement organization and designing, building, reporting of key KPIs to procurement leadership to ensure the success of new procurement operating model.

The GDC SLM_Ops Senior Procurement Manager as part of GDC SLM / Operations Team will be responsible for the delivery of the critical supplier lifecycle management service for multiple categories such as Marketing or Professional Services and more. This role will collaborate with the procurement categories to manage suppliers performance and provide commercial opportunity management.

This service is a cornerstone of the future procurement operating model and will enable the function to industrialise its core operation; driving best practice, providing KPI / performance monitoring and reporting, service delivery and conduct supplier performance management and continual improvement.

The role is also accountable for the transition to, and ongoing delivery of, the future ready procurement operation model as it relates to the GDC, management of bank of service levels for contracting, dashboards, service delivery with excellent communication skills including feedback to categories with clear execution plan driving towards set targets. As such the role is pivotal to the successful execution of the procurement future ready transformation.

Key Responsibilities

Operations Management

Overall responsibility for coordinating site operations for the Global Delivery Centre to ensure the centre is a success and runs smoothly and efficiently.
Provide oversight for key areas of the GDC, including budget management, performance management, talent management, liaising with GPLT, and chairing the GDC operations committee.
Lead a multi-functional matrix-managed team in delivery of all GDC operational activities (e.g., source to contract service delivery teams, performance management, common process design, training etc.)
Provide oversight to ensure there is a strong culture and community within GDC, and it stays aligned with GSK values and expectations.
Collaborate with Global proc leadership team and wider GSK community to determine GDC priorities and share learnings
Ensure procurement Global Delivery leads requests and priorities are met.
Responsible for standing in for Global Delivery Head whenever required (e.g. for reporting to GPLT).
Resolve escalations, including from senior business stakeholders, timely and tactfully
Manage complex prioritizations with multiple variables/considerations including but not limited to stakeholder sensitivity, business value and impact, spend, savings, compliance, risk to GSK etc.
Understand and efficiently navigate organizational complexity to deliver set goals
Engage with the wider GSK project management community and update the GDC community on learnings.

Performance Management

Responsible for managing KPIs, and effectively using them to deliver efficiencies and facilitate improvement. Strong source to contract process understanding is required to design effective KPIs aligned to assurance of supply (risk management), quality, service, cost and innovation
Create and implement the performance management strategy, framework and process for GDC KPIs in collaboration with category and proc leadership teams
Ensure all GDC performance metrics are set in a measurable way and are easy to access/track
Ensure GSK’s Procurement policies, procedures, processes, systems and methodologies are utilized in a compliant manner by GDC
Responsible for Identification of performance issues, root causes and establish training plan to fill gaps along with GDC leadership
Managing Learning & Development needs of GDC by leveraging existing or building new training plans
Ability to create and maintain monthly performance management reports and dashboards and commentary for executive team.
Engage and contribute to the wider GSK performance analysis community

People leadership (for roles with people management responsibilities)

Support GDC Ops lead in ensuring smooth operations and performance management, talent management for GDC
Develop and implement effective ways of working across the GDC, LOC and Category team to ensure a cohesive approach
Develop the team and individual capabilities from both technical and behavioural aspects

Drive operational efficiency

Set and deliver continuous improvement of all process with responsibility to build best in class Global Delivery Centre in Procurement
Ensure that GSK’s procurement policies, procedures, processes, systems and methodologies are utilized in a compliant manner in the area of responsibility

Strategy execution

Align with appropriate Global category teams and work in collaboration with these teams to share insights and practical knowledge to provide supplier performance management and commercial management including diagnosis for commercial opportunities
Lead KPI/ Performance monitoring and reporting, credits management and execution, reporting , rebate collection
Be procurement subject matter experts for one or multiple categories across all spends such as Marketing & Sales or Corporate services and others to commercial opportunities
Manage and conduct periodic contract reviews, expiry and renewal needs of the contracts and input to contracts negotiations with SLA/ KPI metrics

Benefit delivery

Deliver benefits identified in strategies globally, through effective and collaborative contract execution and supplier performance management and commercial management
Monitor KPIs and performance monitoring , report it to Categories,
Manage execution of credits and rebates and report efficiency

Cooperation with Categories

Build strategic relationship with a network of senior Procurement leads to identify opportunities to create value
Create WOW and governance to support categories to conduct regular performance management meetings and realise the commercial opportunities
Understand and efficiently navigate organizational complexity to deliver set goals

Knowledge, Skills & Experience required:

10-15 years of relevant procurement experience in an operational /strategic role in supplier or vendor management in proc operation centre, proc shared service centre, captive centre or capability centre; working with team members across all levels of an organisation.
Strong procurement process and concept understanding including strategic sourcing, sourcing execution, commercial and contract negotiations for complex global indirect categories
Proven ability to manage a large number of complex stakeholders.
Strong understanding of performance management industry standards and tools
Strong analytics skills and understanding of data interpretation and associated tools
Good exposure in previous roles to multi-cultural, multi-functional environment
This role should have strong demonstrated procurement expertise in commercial and contract negotiations, strategic sourcing, cost models synthesis and supplier relationship management for delivering multi-country, high spend complex engagements.
Demonstrated experience in navigating through organizational complexity, collaborating simultaneously with multiple teams, influencing without authority, handling escalations and associated business impact.
Demonstrated experience in understanding and utilising complex business processes in all GSK supplier transactions, including all risk/regulatory/compliance
Strong experience in using tools like Ariba SLM
Ability to identify and escalate roadblocks, risks and issues in a timely and effective manner.
Exceptional people management skills
Senior Leadership Team exposure
Strong understanding of financial performance models. Advanced Analytics qualifications / experience are a plus
Project Management experience or certification is a plus

Why GSK?

Uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together.

GSK is a global biopharma company with a purpose to unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. We aim to positively impact the health of 2.5 billion people by the end of the decade, as a successful, growing company where people can thrive. We get ahead of disease by preventing and treating it with innovation in specialty medicines and vaccines. We focus on four therapeutic areas: respiratory, immunology and inflammation; oncology; HIV; and infectious diseases – to impact health at scale.

People and patients around the world count on the medicines and vaccines we make, so we’re committed to creating an environment where our people can thrive and focus on what matters most. Our culture of being ambitious for patients, accountable for impact and doing the right thing is the foundation for how, together, we deliver for patients, shareholders and our people.

GSK does not accept referrals from employment businesses and/or employment agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site. All employment businesses/agencies are required to contact GSKs commercial and general procurement/human resources department to obtain prior written authorization before referring any candidates to GSK. The obtaining of prior written authorization is a condition precedent to any agreement (verbal or written) between the employment business/ agency and GSK. In the absence of such written authorization being obtained any actions undertaken by the employment business/agency shall be deemed to have been performed without the consent or contractual agreement of GSK. GSK shall therefore not be liable for any fees arising from such actions or any fees arising from any referrals by employment businesses/agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site.

Job Description

Intuit Global Sourcing & Procurement (GSP) is transforming Source-to-Report processes and technology to create a seamless digital procurement experience for employees, enhance performance in our strategic partners, and amplify value delivered for One Intuit. As we adapt to the changing demands of our procurement landscape with controls, compliance and talent considerations, and as we position our reimagined procurement organization to enable both Intuit’s evolution as an AI-driven expert platform and Finance’s transformation to accelerate sustainable value, the Shared Services Group Manager will play a pivotal role leading the overall strategy and operating rhythm of our move to a dedicated capability center focused on the effectiveness, efficiency and experience utilizing both in-house and third party talent to execute business processes across procurement.

The Shared Services Group Manager will serve as a strategic leader and steward, requiring strong business acumen and knowledge of the procurement function and its supporting business processes, with experience operationalizing shared services to effectively consolidate, optimize, standardize and transform our processes, while bringing continuous improvements and best practices to bear based on internal/external insights and performance management of talent. Key responsibilities include developing and operationalizing the target state operating model taking full advantage of third party staff augmentation while evaluating optimization opportunities leveraging in-house employees, partnering with procurement process owners and subject matter experts to standardize and achieve maximum effectiveness and efficiency with current state outsourced service provider talent, while shaping the future state automation roadmap drawing on synergies across the Intuit ecosystem with AI-assisted experiences. Operational leadership of Intuit’s procurement function demands a proactive self-starter capable of driving change. The outcomes significantly impact crucial corporate-level objectives, influencing various business units and functional groups within Intuit.

Responsibilities

Leadership and Strategy:

Cultivates and coordinates relationships with Sourcing, Procurement, Data & Automation, Controls & Compliance, and Finance stakeholders, with ability to interact with senior leaders to provide strategic guidance or support decision-making.
Adept at assessing current state staffing composition, exploring various degrees of captive center vs. OSP strategy, and making recommendations based on a transformational vision.
Develops and executes the vision for a best in class shared services procurement program, keeping the customer experience at the forefront while seeking automation and AI-assisted solutions as velocity drivers for GSP. Develops the plan, operational roadmap, and goals in service to that vision.
Creates buoyancy for Sourcing, Procurement and Travel & Expense business process owners, acting as the local oversight for OSPs/employees while enabling process owners to maintain governance of the process.
Collaborates across Intuit to leverage resources and knowledge to solve problems and achieve results.
Role-models execution with velocity, agility, and excellence.
Partners with international teams to ensure globally compliant and locally relevant policies and procedures.

Designs, builds and implements the operating model necessary to realize GSP’s target state, including optimizing the current state to free-up high value resources, developing the strategy to reduce OSP footprint, evolving the shared services center to a follow the sun model, and driving transactional work to AI/automation.
Responsible for the development, management, optimization and scaling of partner performance management of our OSPs, including budget/forecast management and staffing plans.
Ensures quality, velocity and compliance of procurement standard operating procedures including purchase order and invoice processing, expense auditing, and help desk operations, fostering a culture of excellence and innovation within the organization.
Provides direct management and oversight of the procurement help desk including the development and optimization of our knowledge base that enables both AI-assisted chat and our ability to get end users in the hands of human experts when they need it.
Identifies potential risks, ensuring compliance with company policies, and preventing unauthorized expenditures.
Collaborates with GSP International Procurement, Controls & Compliance, International Accounting and Compliance and other business partners to support regulatory compliance reporting and audits as required.

People Management:

Provides oversight and local management of ~6 teams comprised of 50+ contingent workers supporting critical business operations

Job Description

Amazon strives to be Earths most customer-centric company where people can find and discover virtually anything they want to buy online. By giving customers more of what they want – low prices, vast selection, and convenience – Amazon continues to grow and evolve as a world-class e-commerce platform.

Amazon Retail India Pvt Ltd (ARIPL) is hiring an Instock Manager to manage Dry Grocery business. Instock Manager will be responsible for the availability of our selection to customers: “what you do not have in stock, you cannot sell”. This role will comprise demand planning, ordering, inventory management and setting up new processes to scale up as we plan to grow multi-fold in next few years. He/ She will work closely with multiple teams to create the optimal experience for the customer in a scalable way. The individual will track key metrics and drive improvements through actionable plans. He/ She will provide leadership on supply chain topics and ensure alignment with business strategy.

The right candidate for this position should enjoy working in a start-up environment where collaboration, flexibility and a sense of humor are highly valued.

Key job responsibilities

Key job responsibilities

Manage inventory availability and inventory health including: improved in-stock rates, inventory planning and systematic forecasting.
Partner with internal teams to help create and drive tools and process improvements that affect purchasing and vendor management workflow
Drive process improvement and operational excellence within our vendor community by reducing costs, defects, and lead times, while increasing innovation, automation, and economies of scale
Partner with vendor managers in driving sales and profitability growth
Drive complex business analysis that identifies opportunities to improve cost structure and profitability

Basic Qualifications

3+ years of with Excel experience
2+ years of supply chain, inventory management or project management experience
Bachelors degree in operations, supply chain or logistics

Preferred Qualifications

Knowledge of the principles of statistical inventory control
Experience in Retail or wholesale business

The Category Buyer - Services is responsible for supporting the business needs by ensuring timely procurement of requests related to assigned categories in Services. He /She ensures that the category performance is in alignment with the guidelines and targets set under SCM objectives. He /She adheres to the policy guidelines and organizational processes defined for procurement.

The Category Buyer performs sourcing for sub-categories under the Services category, amounting to ~$100 - 110 Mn spend. He/ She assists demand forecasting and annual sourcing plan creation, aligned with the business requirements. He /She analyses the supply market and industry trends and assists the Category Lead in developing category strategies.

He /She executes sourcing and negotiation strategies basis category strategy for all the major sourcing projects. He /She is an effective negotiator who can come up with creative negotiation strategies. He /She monitors contractual compliance and coordinates contract amendments (if any).

He/ She creates catalog for direct purchase and promotes catalog based ordering amongst all business functions. He /She collaborates with the vendors for optimizing technical specifications of products/ services. He /She is responsible for effective supplier relationship management and leads collaborative discussions for value creation with the supplier. He /She maintains a good working relationships with all business functions, SCM Operations, SCM Enablers and SCM Logistics to ensure cross-functional collaboration.

He /She is accountable for compliance to the guidelines and rules, both internal and external (tax laws, legal/ regulatory laws), and accountable for any artefacts/ processes needed for the same.

Examine supplier's industry position and cost-structure, identify trends in supply market, develop cost-tear downs for key sourcing requests
Optimize sourcing and ordering schedule by assessing demand drivers
Minimize the potential risks to reduce supply chain uncertainties and shocks (through laid-out risk mitigation strategies)

Cross Functional Collaboration

General Management

Location: Assam/Jagiroad

Role Overview

The Supplier Quality & Supplier Management Lead is responsible for driving supplier quality excellence, performance management, and risk mitigation across semiconductor backend manufacturing operations. This role works closely with procurement, engineering, and manufacturing teams to qualify suppliers, establish robust quality systems, and continuously improve supplier performance. The position also plays a key leadership role in supplier development, business continuity, and building a strong zero‑defect culture.

Key Responsibilities

Develop, implement, and sustain supplier quality assurance programs to ensure reliable and consistent component quality. 
Define, track, and drive supplier performance through clearly established quality metrics and KPIs. 
Lead Quality Business Reviews (QBRs) with suppliers and ensure timely closure of improvement actions. 
Maintain effective communication channels with suppliers to address quality concerns, provide performance feedback, and strengthen collaboration. 
Lead supplier qualification and approval processes, including documentation review, capability assessments, and on‑site audits. 
Conduct supplier audits to evaluate compliance with quality systems, specifications, and applicable regulatory requirements. 
Perform supplier quality risk assessments using FMEA and other risk‑based methodologies. 
Ensure supplier alignment with industry quality standards such as ISO 9001, IATF 16949, and ISO 26262. 
Drive supplier development initiatives focused on quality improvement, cost efficiency, and overall supply chain performance. 
Evaluate and onboard new suppliers to support business continuity and mitigate supply risk. 
Develop and maintain supplier quality agreements outlining quality expectations, roles, and responsibilities. 
Lead structured problem‑solving activities using 8D methodology, Control Plans, and corrective/preventive action systems. 
Collaborate with procurement, engineering, and manufacturing teams to resolve supplier quality issues and support sourcing decisions. 
Provide training and guidance to internal teams on supplier quality processes, tools, and best practices. 
Monitor industry trends, regulatory requirements, and supplier performance data to continuously enhance supplier quality strategies. 
Lead and mentor supplier quality teams, fostering accountability, consistency in behavior, and high performance. 
Promote a strong culture of ownership, continuous improvement, and commitment to zero defects. 
Apply strong leadership, communication, and influencing skills to align stakeholders and suppliers to quality objectives. 

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Supply Chain Management, or a related discipline (advanced degree preferred).
15+ years of relevant experience in supplier quality, supplier management, or quality leadership roles within semiconductor or high‑technology manufacturing environments.
Certification in quality management (e.g., ASQ Certified Quality Engineer) is an advantage.

Technical Expertise

Strong understanding of semiconductor assembly and test / backend manufacturing processes.
In‑depth knowledge of quality management systems and industry standards, including ISO 9001, IATF 16949, and ISO 26262.
Familiarity with regulatory requirements such as RoHS and REACH.
Expert‑level proficiency in structured problem solving (8D), FMEA, Control Plans, and process stability methodologies.

Core Competencies

Supplier negotiation and relationship management
Strategic and risk‑based thinking
Influencing across cross‑functional and external stakeholders
Clear, consistent, and effective communication
Visionary leadership with strong execution focus
Deep commitment to zero‑defect quality performance

Job Title: Asst Procurement Manager, Flexible Packaging - India

Department: Procurement

Profession: Supply Chain

Location: Mumbai,

Main Job Purpose

Flexible packaging is a € 200m spend portfolio and a critical growth and value driver for Unilever, representing almost 50% of packaging spend in India. As Unilever advances its commitments on sustainability, recyclability, and end-to-end value chain resilience, the Flexibles Procurement team plays a pivotal role in delivering competitive costs, reliable supply, innovation readiness, desirability and responsible sourcing.

It is a techno-commercial position requiring strong understanding of packaging materials, supplier capabilities, cost structures, and supply chain dynamics. The role ensures executional excellence across sourcing, contracting, supplier development, and portfolio governance while contributing to the broader flexible’s strategy. This role also helps the overall business with appropriate pricing signals to stay competitive in market.

Key Accountabilities

Category & Supplier Management

Execute sourcing strategies for flexibles packaging aligned with regional and global procurement frameworks.

Lead day to day supplier management and negotiations including performance reviews, capability assessments, and development plans.

Maintain strong external relationships to ensure consistent service, quality, and innovation support.

Contracting & Pricing

Drive timely contracting cycles and quarterly price rollouts to ensure OTIF ordering for factories.

Build and validate cost models factoring in feedstock trends, inflation drivers, and supplier quotations.

Maintain strong coordination with Finance on price confirmation and GM impacts.

Cost Efficiency & Value Delivery

Deliver savings through 5S, ZBB initiatives, alternative sourcing, specification harmonization, and NMCI mitigation.

Track, report, and validate value delivery metrics in collaboration with Finance and global reporting teams.

Operations, Forecasting & Reporting

Support forecasting cycles for India and contribute to South Asia-level procurement reporting.

Ensure data accuracy, timely submission, and robust analysis to guide category decisions.

Manage portfolio dashboards, trackers, and performance indicators.

Innovation

Support innovation projects and new networks by ensuring packaging readiness.

Work closely with R&D, Brand, SUIT, Planning, and Factory teams to enable seamless landing of new innovations.

Sustainability & Compliance

Support Unilever’s sustainability commitments including recyclability, plastic reduction, and carbon footprint improvement.

Drive supplier adherence to Responsible Sourcing Policies (RSP) and ethical procurement norms.

Professional Skills

Strong commercial acumen with ability to negotiate, analyse cost structures, and assess supplier competitiveness.
Good understanding of supply chain and manufacturing processes.

Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, especially in cross-functional setups.
Ability to work with ambiguity, drive clarity, and manage multiple priorities.

Your role is to source Fragrances and Flavors aligned to the Business Group and Supply Chain Strategy for Personal Care. You will be responsible for leading the innovation agenda, Focus on desire at scale and unmissable product superiority in the Fragrances & Flavors portfolio for the Personal Care Business Groups. You need to ensure you have best in class service, partnerships, the most competitive prices and genuinely committed to Unilever’s sustainably led business model, connected with Unilever and the business agenda.

The role is based in Mumbai, India and reports into the Fragrance & Flavors Procurement Manager. You will work in close partnership with the Global Manager for PC Fragrances and Oral Care Flavors

Responsibilities Of The Role

You are a strategy Champion: You lead the co-creation of the innovation agenda for your portfolio. You monitor and understand the dynamically changing consumer market, ensuring a future fit trend driven portfolio, delivering desirability at scale and affordable superiority. With a focus on emerging technologies, you lead the development of not only the current portfolio, but future capabilities.
You are external facing and an expert in the supplier landscape: You assess, identify, and select the appropriate supplier base that meet the needs of the business. You ensure and manage overall performance of suppliers and surpass stakeholder needs.
You take considered decisions under pressure: You handle complex and diverse customer, supplier, supply chain or operational issues within generally defined practices and policies in selecting methods and techniques for obtaining solutions.
You are a leader: You are a leader of procurement projects in your portfolio and for your materials. You take on a role leading portfolio wide activity such as accurate price forecasting and complexity reduction.
You are a storyteller: You use your strategic influencing skills to drive Procurement considerations and strategic recommendations with Stakeholders.
You create growth for Unilever: You work closely with Brand, R&D, Supply Chain, Strategy and Insights and Category Procurement teams to support the launch of innovations and new product development.
You achieve key financial targets: Delivering the business financial targets for Buying Performance, Product Superiority and Complexity reduction for the portfolio materials/services and suppliers. You prepare information on purchase prices and conditions to enable proper budgeting and cash forecasting.

Key Skills

To drive additional value from the unique position you are in, you need to be excellent in:

Procurement Sourcing Strategy – (level 2 - Capable)

Ability to find, evaluate and select the best suppliers to drive savings, increase value and manage risk.

Supplier Analysis – (level 3 - Intermediate)

Ability to assess the weaknesses and strengths of prospective and current suppliers with respect to their – availability of capable staff, business model, capacity, sales revenue, stocks, reputation, mark-ups, mark-downs, gross margins, quality, service, reliability, pricing policies, payment terms, etc.

Value Chain Understanding – (level 3 - Intermediate)

A value chain is a set of activities that an organization carries out to create value for its customers. Value Chain understanding is the ability to understand how the company makes value, by understanding all the activities and how they connect. Understanding the value chain allows us to look for ways to add more value.

Supplier Negotiation – (level 3 - Intermediate)

Successful negotiations are not a win-lose proposition. The best negotiations result in a win for all parties, which leads to stronger relationships and a desire to continue a partnership that is mutually beneficial.

During the procurement process, a negotiation between corporate buyers and sellers is the first step in developing a supply contract which has a direct effect on profit or loss. To be a good negotiator you need to: prepare and plan, be knowledgeable about the subject matter negotiated; be able to think clearly and rapidly under pressure and uncertainty; be able to express thoughts verbally; be a good listener; have sound judgment and integrity; and be able to persuade others.

Contract Negotiation – (level 2 - Capable)

Contract negotiation is the process of coming to an agreement on a set of legally binding terms. When two companies negotiate, both parties seek to obtain favorable terms and minimize financial, legal, and operational risk.

Sourcing Resilience – (level 3 - Intermediate)

Ability to run procurement operations to focus not only on 'just in time' but also on 'just in case', by anticipating and planning for uncertainty. It does involve finding robust alternative suppliers and anticipate situations that can have an impact on such as: out of stock, bankruptcy, climate issues...

Stakeholder Management – (level 3 - Intermediate)

Ability to identify and then understand the motivations and behaviors of anyone who can affect what you are trying to achieve on a project; then developing relevant strategies to influence outcomes.

Strategic Partnerships – (level 3 - Intermediate)

Ability to join forces with like-minded strategic partners to reach objectives that would be unattainable if going solo.

Ability to adopt a deliberate approach to ensure the planned benefits of the relationship are achieved. This includes:

▪ developing and maintaining strong individual relationships with your strategic partner and its stakeholders

▪ obtaining a deep analytical understanding of your strategic partner and the relationship

▪ defining a clear strategy & plan for the strategic relationship

▪ ensuring a strong governance, monitoring, and evaluation of the strategic relationship

▪ and regularly refreshing and adapting your approach to the strategic relationship

Collaboration – (level 3 - Intermediate)

Enables people within an organization or outside an organization to engage with each other productively and efficiently.

Program Management (level 3 - Intermediate)

Lead the planning and implementation of key change programs.

Additional Skills

…. related below core procurement skills:

Contract Management (level 2 - Capable)
Supplier Relationship Management (level 3 - Intermediate)

At Unilever, your career will be a unique journey, grounded in our inclusive, collaborative, and flexible working environment. We don’t believe in the ‘one size fits all’ approach and instead we will equip you with the tools you need to shape your own future.

Job Purpose

Unilever is the place where you can bring your purpose to life with the work that you do – creating a better business and a better world.

Strategy & Insights Is The Nerve Centre Of Our Procurement Operations Globally. The Global S&I Manager Works Very Closely With The BG PC Procurement Leadership Team And Acts As a Healthy Forward-looking Challenger, As Well As a Partner To The Personal Care Team, Provoking And Bringing New Thinking. With The Following Key Activities

Facilitate creation and updating of portfolio strategies and deliver analytics and guidance for negotiations and contracting applying expertise and the newly created playbooks, incorporating best-in-class solutions.
Lead the Insights creation from available data, based on a good understanding of the data architecture and guiding the mining process
Help define and Rollout strategic transformation programs, such as Price Transparency and ZBB
Derive value creation opportunities based on deep Value Chain expertise, Supplier, Competitor and Industry Insights, as well as benchmarking and cost modelling

Key Responsibilities

Facilitate creation and updating of portfolio strategies, and deliver analytics and guidance for negotiations and contracting applying expertise and the newly created playbooks, incorporating best-in-class solutions

Co-leads with the sourcing team the key strategic activities within the portfolio, acting as a challenger, bringing fresh thinking and ideas and balancing historical knowledge
Masters the newly created frameworks/playbooks on key procurement skills (Strategy, Negotiation, Contracting), acting as consultant to the portfolio, using the tools to upskill the sourcing team and as enablers for the execution.

Lead the Insights creation from available data, based on a good understanding of the data architecture and guiding the mining process
